growth_records.strain: Rm1021 (bacteroid, in symbiosis with Medicago truncatula)
growth_records.medium: In planta (nitrogen-fixing zone III nodule bacteroid); not a defined culture medium

up.method: experimental transport/uptake measurement CITED from prior literature (not measured in this paper); values reported as experimentally determined uptake rates by S. meliloti bacteroids, used to validate the ViNE FBA prediction of 1.3 mmol/gDW/h

growth_records.cite: diCenzo GC et al. Genome-scale metabolic reconstruction of the symbiosis between a leguminous plant and a nitrogen-fixing bacterium. Nature Communications (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41467-020-16484-2. [PMC7244743]
growth_records.snippet: predicted C4-dicarboxylate import by N2-fixing bacteroids (1.3 mmol h-1 [g bacteroid dry weight]-1) was similar to experimentally determined uptake rates by S. meliloti bacteroids (1.1 to 1.3 mmol h-1 [g bacteroid dry weight]-1)
growth_records.notes: This is a genome-scale metabolic reconstruction/FBA paper (ViNE, S. meliloti nodulating M. truncatula); virtually all quantitative rates are FBA-PREDICTED, not measured. The ONLY experimental prokaryote rate mentioned is the C4-dicarboxylate uptake of 1.1-1.3 mmol/gDW/h by S. meliloti bacteroids, and it is CITED from prior literature (references 35 = Zhao H et al.; 91 = McRae DG, Miller RW, Berndt WB, Joy K, 'Transport of C4-dicarboxylates...'), NOT generated in this study. Recorded here only because the task asks to capture cited experimental rates and flag the citation. No defined medium, temperature, pH, or growth rate is given for this bacteroid condition. Other numbers in the paper are model outputs and were deliberately NOT recorded as experimental: FBA specific-growth-rate reductions of ~0.043 h-1 (glucose) and ~0.030 h-1 (succinate) from adding NGAM (predicted); FBA plant growth ~0.044 g/day/gDW and N2-fixation ~3 umol/h/g plant DW (predicted); N2-fixation efficiency constant 150 umol/h/g nodule DW (model parameter). Cited plant (Medicago sativa) values (growth ~0.1 g/day/gDW; N2-fixation ~7 umol/h/g by acetylene reduction; carbon cost 3-5 g C/g N) are for the PLANT, not a prokaryote, and are cited, so excluded.
